mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates robust real-sector demand and business investment, which jointly support corporate revenue growth and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the industrial production index reflects supply-side bottlenecks and declining manufacturing output, which erodes corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive during consecutive monthly declines in industrial production, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in residential fixed investment indicates collapsing housing demand and credit tightening, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite consecutive monthly drops in residential fixed investment, or the signal generates a negative information coefficient over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if real GDP growth remains positive for three consecutive quarters while the S&P 500 delivers negative cumulative forward returns over the same period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in non-farm payroll growth signals stagflationary pressure, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict downward equity moves when inflation rises and employment growth slows, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below the 1100 thousand unit threshold indicates a structural downturn in the construction sector and consumer confidence, which triggers risk aversion and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when housing starts persistently trade below 1100 thousand units, or the trigger generates a false positive rate exceeding 70% over a 3-year backtest.
